scenario: A local macOS app shell lets a user search Codex/OpenClaw/future Claude Code sessions through the same public-safe recall surfaces, inspect safe summaries and source refs, filter by project/status/priority, and hand high-signal refs to an agent without raw transcript rendering.
expected_public_safe_evidence: query string with no private transcript text
live tool names when connected through CLI, MCP, or OpenClaw gateway
local_mac_shell_ready runtime marker when claiming connected local Mac UI proof
project, status, and priority filter state
selected bounded expansion profile and token budget
desktop live/no-focus proof harness blockers or readiness status
desktop GUI proof-report blocker or approval-fixture status
blocker codes for unavailable local DB or plugin tools
pass_criteria: User can search indexed sessions and see safe summaries with source refs.
User can filter results by project, status, priority, and blocker state.
User can copy source refs and selected public-safe summary text for an OpenClaw agent.
User can request bounded expansion through existing loo_* profiles before more detail is shown.
The live CLI proof mode records read-only loo_* tool source metadata without raw transcript rendering.
The connected runtime proof only clears local_mac_shell_ready when the shell is actually ready on macOS.
The shell must fail closed when local DB access, required plugin tools, or OpenClaw plugin loading is unavailable.
CUA and Peekaboo appear as readiness/status surfaces only unless a future backend-specific approval gate is proven.
Desktop live/no-focus proof harness output prepares an action-bound proof plan without performing GUI mutation.
Desktop GUI proof-report output validates supplied public-safe observations without itself performing GUI mutation.
fail_criteria: UI renders raw Codex transcripts or raw prompts.
UI captures screenshots or videos as public evidence.
UI performs live GUI mutation or live Codex control without explicit approval.
UI claims one-click install, Claude Code adapter parity, unattended takeover, or release-ready macOS app status.
UI falls back to raw file reads when plugin tools or the local DB are unavailable.
known_gaps: The repo includes a static prototype shell and live CLI proof mode, but no packaged macOS app shell has been produced.
No signed or notarized app artifact has been produced.
No live OpenClaw gateway UI event-loop smoke has been run.
A Peekaboo scratch-window no-focus proof exists for one approved TextEdit window move; this does not prove generic GUI mutation or Codex GUI mutation.
A CUA Driver scratch-window no-focus proof exists for one approved TextEdit launch_app action; this does not prove generic GUI mutation or Codex GUI mutation.
Claude Code remains a future adapter boundary, not a proved equivalent adapter.
next_action: Run the live CLI UI proof against the local orchestrator DB, then decide whether the next proof should be OpenClaw gateway UI event-loop smoke or a packaged macOS wrapper.
proof_boundary: This advisory local public-safe beta scorecard records a Peekaboo scratch-window proof and a CUA Driver scratch-window no-focus proof for one approved TextEdit launch_app action, not generic GUI mutation. It does not prove a signed or release-ready macOS app, one-click install, Claude Code adapter parity, live Codex control, Codex GUI mutation, or safe desktop automation.
